query_normalized: exploring the validity of the weak equivalence principle with publicly available planetary orbital data physics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it provides sensitive tests of the Equivalence Principle (specifically the Weak Equivalence Principle via the PPN parameter $\beta$) using Lunar Laser Ranging data, which constitutes a canonical empirical study of the exact mechanism and domain (gravitational physics with orbital data) the user is investigating.
